Class GameTimer

java.lang.Object
com.example.escriturarapida.model.timer.GameTimer
All Implemented Interfaces:
IGameTimer
public class GameTimer extends Object implements IGameTimer
Manages the countdown timer for each game level.

Uses a JavaFX Timeline to decrement the remaining time every second, update the timer label with visual feedback, and trigger a callback when the time reaches zero. Implements IGameTimer to define the required timer operations.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Cristian Camilo Criollo Diaz

  • Constructor Details

    • GameTimer

      public GameTimer(javafx.scene.control.Label timerLabel, Consumer<Integer> onTick, Runnable onFinish)
      Constructs a new GameTimer with the specified UI label and callbacks.
      Parameters:
      timerLabel - the Label that displays the countdown
      onTick - a Consumer called each second with the remaining time; can be a no-op if not needed
      onFinish - a Runnable called when the timer reaches zero

  • Method Details

    • startTimer

      public void startTimer(int tiempoInicial)
      Starts the countdown timer from the given initial time.

      Stops any currently running timer before starting a new one. The timer decrements every second, updates the UI, and calls onFinish when it reaches zero.

      Specified by:
      startTimer in interface IGameTimer
      Parameters:
      tiempoInicial - the starting time in seconds

    • updateUI

      private void updateUI()
      Updates the timer label text and applies visual styles based on urgency.

      When 10 or fewer seconds remain, the label alternates between red bold and white normal style every second to create a blinking warning effect. Above 10 seconds, the label is displayed in white normal style.

    • getTiempoRestante

      public int getTiempoRestante()
      Returns the number of seconds remaining in the current countdown.
      Specified by:
      getTiempoRestante in interface IGameTimer
      Returns:
      the remaining time in seconds

    • stopTimer

      public void stopTimer()
      Stops the countdown timer if it is currently running.
      Specified by:
      stopTimer in interface IGameTimer
